hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network is first pretrained on a data-rich domain of additive-free SEM images and then fine-tuned on a smaller data-poor domain of additive-containing samples, then the two-stage transfer learning approach will achieve higher classification accuracy for distinguishing NCM cathode compositions and aging states compared to fine-tuning without pretraining or pretraining alone, because the source domain pretraining enables the model to learn transferable morphological feature representations that compensate for the scarcity of labeled target domain data.
